facts.text: Leesburg was first settled in 1857 by Evander McIver Lee, whose brother John Calvin Lee later gave the town its name.
The city incorporated in 1875, just a decade after Lake County was formed, and the Leesburg Public Library was founded the same year.
Leesburg’s Venetian Gardens Park, built as a WPA project during the Great Depression, still offers walkways, a marina, and a baseball field.
The library moved to Venetian Gardens in 1953 and, in 2007, opened a 42,000‑square‑foot building that now serves the entire Lake County Library System.
Between Lake Harris and Lake Griffin, Leesburg sits at the head of the Ocklawaha River, giving it a distinctive central‑Florida waterway setting.
Leesburg once led the world in citrus production, but back‑to‑back freezes in 1894–95 nearly wiped out the industry before it rebounded in the 20th century.
